annotations.label: Mathematical physics

anchor.snippet: Mathematical physics is the development of mathematical methods for use in physics and their applications

annotations.note: A meta-level description of a discipline, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Physical mathematics

anchor.snippet: known as physical mathematics

annotations.note: A naming of a field, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Noether's theorem

anchor.snippet: as embodied within the most elementary formulation of Noether's theorem

annotations.note: The physics symmetry-to-conservation-law theorem has no formalization in Mathlib (matches for 'Noether' are unrelated Noetherian ring/space theory).

annotations.label: Kepler's laws of planetary motion

anchor.snippet: formalized in the equations of Kepler's laws of planetary motion

annotations.note: No declaration matching Kepler's laws of planetary motion exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galileo's law of free fall and inertia

anchor.snippet: established the law of equal free fall as well as the principles of inertial motion

annotations.note: A physical law of motion with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Galilean invariance

anchor.snippet: the principle of Galilean invariance , also called Galilean relativity

annotations.note: A physics invariance principle not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus

anchor.snippet: important concepts such as the fundamental theorem of calculus

annotations.note: The FTC is directly formalized as integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt in the FundThmCalculus file.

annotations.label: Fermat's theorem on stationary points

anchor.snippet: finding extrema and minima of functions via differentiation using Fermat's theorem

annotations.note: Fermat's stationary-point theorem is formalized as IsLocalExtr.deriv_eq_zero (a local extremum has vanishing derivative).

annotations.label: Newton's laws of motion and universal gravitation

anchor.snippet: modeled three Galilean laws of motion along with Newton's law of universal gravitation

annotations.note: Physical laws of motion and gravitation with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Fourier series

anchor.snippet: introduced the notion of Fourier series to solve the heat equation

annotations.note: Fourier series are formalized via the fourier basis and fourierCoeff coefficients on the additive circle.

annotations.label: Maxwell's equations

anchor.snippet: whittled down by others to the four Maxwell's equations

annotations.note: The electromagnetic field equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galilean transformation

anchor.snippet: The Galilean transformation had been the mathematical process used to translate the positions in one reference frame

annotations.note: No formalization of the Galilean transformation between reference frames exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentz transformation

anchor.snippet: this process was replaced by Lorentz transformation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Lorentz transformation; matches for 'Lorentz' are unrelated.

annotations.label: Minkowski spacetime

anchor.snippet: applied linear algebra to model 3D space together with the 1D axis of time

annotations.note: Minkowski spacetime is absent; the 'Minkowski' hits are Minkowski's convex-body theorem in geometry of numbers, a different result.

annotations.label: Lorentz contraction

anchor.snippet: as modeled in the Lorentz contraction

annotations.note: The relativistic length contraction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Special theory of relativity

anchor.snippet: Albert Einstein published his special theory of relativity

annotations.note: Special relativity as a physical theory has no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: extended curved geometry to N dimensions with differential geometry , now known as Riemannian geometry

annotations.note: The core objects of Riemannian geometry are formalized via RiemannianMetric and the IsRiemannianManifold class.

annotations.label: Geodesic motion in general relativity

anchor.snippet: free falling particles with mass move along a geodesic curve in the spacetime

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geodesic definition or GR geodesic equation (geodesic matches are unrelated graph-theory terms).

annotations.label: Quantization

anchor.snippet: He introduced the first non-naïve definition of quantization in this paper

annotations.note: The physics notion of quantization is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quantum framework of self-adjoint operators

anchor.snippet: based on a probabilistic interpretation of states, and evolution and measurements in terms of self-adjoint operators on an infinite-dimensional vector space

annotations.note: Self-adjoint operators (IsSelfAdjoint) are formalized, but the probabilistic quantum-measurement framework built on them is not.

annotations.label: Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: That is called Hilbert space

annotations.note: A Hilbert space is directly formalized as a complete normed inner product space via the HilbertSpace structure.

annotations.label: Spectral theory

anchor.snippet: he built up a relevant part of modern functional analysis on Hilbert spaces, the spectral theory

annotations.note: Spectral theory's core is formalized via the spectrum definition and the spectral theorem for self-adjoint operators (LinearMap.IsSymmetric in InnerProductSpace.Spectrum).
